LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY SUMMARY JUDGMENT FOR SALE AND PARTITION WAS HEARD AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 12/30/03. RULING: THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN INTERLOCUTORY ORDER CALLING FOR THE SALE AND PARTITION OF THE PROPERTY LOCATED AT 1051 NOE STREET/497 ELIZABETH STREET, SAN FRANCISCO. COUNSEL ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME FOR THE PURPOSE OF AGREEING ON A REFEREE WHO MAY BE USED TO WORK OUT THE DETAILS OF THE SALE. SHOULD COUNSEL, IN THE EXERCISE OF GOOD FAITH, BE UNABLE TO AGREE UPON A REFEREE, THEY MAY APPLY TO THIS COURT FOR AN APPOINTMENT OF AN APPROPRIATE PERSON. (301/JLW)

Off Calendar Sep-25-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew M. Zacks, Esq. and James Brandan Krause, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF ANDREW M. ZACKS 956-8100, for cross-defendant Paul Nicola, moving for a special motion to strike
